items.title: Easy editor to write your tests
items.summary: An editor right inside trace. Colours and instant feedback while you type, so you spot mistakes early.
items.notes: Monaco-based, custom Gherkin grammar. Parse errors via @cucumber/gherkin in src/lib/editor.

items.title: Short codes for each test
items.summary: Every test gets a short code like AUTH-42. Quick to mention in chat or in a meeting. The link keeps working even if you rename the test later.
items.notes: projects.slug (URL, unique global, regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$, length 2..40), projects.code_prefix (display, unique per project, same regex, length 2..15), features.code_seq (int sequence per project — Postgres sequence or MAX(seq)+1 under advisory lock). Routing: /p/<slug>/<feature_code>; uuid never in URLs. Both slug + code_prefix locked after creation — typo means new project (trade-off: shared client/IVV links never die). Create form auto-suggests both from project name (slug = kebab-case the name; code_prefix = first slug segment, or full slug if 1–2 segments), visible + editable before submit, then frozen. Code stable across archive/delete. Rendered everywhere user reads a feature (sidebar tree, history rows, cockpit header, CSV, HTML report). Command palette indexes the code (AUTH-42 jumps). CI ingest: optional X-CI-Feature-Code header takes precedence over feature name. Backfill on migration derives both from projects.name, slug collision-safe with -2/-3 suffix. Schema in drizzle/0002. Out of scope: cross-project codes, custom code patterns (AUTH-FEAT-42 vs AUTH-42), code reservation for unborn features, slug/prefix rename with alias history.

items.title: Sort your tests into folders
items.summary: Put related tests in named folders. Drag them around to reorder. Everyone on the team sees the same order.
items.notes: feature_groups + position column. Drag via svelte-dnd-action.

items.title: Walk through a test live
items.summary: Click through a test step by step as you run it. Mark each step pass, fail, or skip. Drop in screenshots or short notes when something looks off.
items.notes: Verdict shortcuts, S3-backed attachments, per-scenario notes. See src/lib/widgets/cockpit.

items.title: Stop a run in the middle
items.summary: Need to cancel a run halfway? One click and the rest of the steps are marked skipped, so the run closes cleanly.
items.notes: Abort flow shipped without Pause (PAUSED deferred — see [[pause-running-run]]). Marks pending scenarios SKIPPED, execution ABORTED.

items.title: Re-run only the failed steps
items.summary: When part of a run fails, re-run just those steps without redoing the ones that already passed.
items.notes: Restart a new execution scoped to scenarios with verdict=fail from the source execution.

items.title: Project dashboard at a glance
items.summary: See your project health in one screen: pass rate over the last week, runs today, and where the action's been.
items.notes: src/routes/(app)/p/[slug]/+page. Tiles: pass rate 7d, runs today, etc. dash_* i18n keys.

items.title: Quick search with one keystroke
items.summary: Press Cmd+K (or Ctrl+K) to jump to any test from anywhere in trace. Type a name, a code, hit enter.
items.notes: Topbar CommandPalette widget. Indexes features, project search. Also opens with '/'.

items.title: Light, dark, and accent colours
items.summary: Switch between light and dark mode any time. Pick the accent colour that feels right (pink, amber, cyan, lime).
items.notes: Cookie-backed theme + accent. Tokens in src/app.css. Account settings page exposes the picker.

items.title: Ready-to-explore demo project on day one
items.summary: When you first sign up, you get a sample project with real-looking tests and a past run so you can poke around right away.
items.notes: src/lib/server/onboarding. seedDemoProject on first admin signup. WelcomeCard widget guides the 3-step tour.

items.title: Collect results from automated tests
items.summary: If your team runs tests automatically (after a deploy, every night, on every change), trace picks up the results and shows them here.
items.notes: POST /api/executions/ingest, project API key auth. Headers: X-Project-Id, X-Environment, X-CI-Source/Branch/Commit.

items.title: Know where each result came from
items.summary: Every run remembers which version of the app was tested. Search old runs by version, branch, or where they ran.
items.notes: execution.environment / ci_branch / ci_commit / ci_source columns. drizzle/0002. Surfaces in history filters.

items.title: Find old runs, spot tests that flip-flop
items.summary: Look back through past runs and narrow the list down. Tests that pass one day and fail the next get flagged for you. Save the list as a spreadsheet anytime.
items.notes: Filters chained via URL params. Flake detection on scenario verdict alternation. CSV export endpoint.

items.title: Move your tests in and out
items.summary: Drag in a folder of test files to add them all at once. Or download a whole project as one file you can send to someone or keep as a backup.
items.notes: .feature zip round-trip via archiver. Group meta preserved. src/lib/server/import + features/export.

items.title: Sign in with your work account
items.summary: Use the same login as your other work tools. Email and password still works too. (Some specific providers still need real-world testing.)
items.notes: better-auth genericOAuth plugin. OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL + CLIENT_ID + CLIENT_SECRET. Keycloak not yet tested end-to-end; verify before promising it publicly.

items.title: Private keys for your automated tools
items.summary: Each project has its own private keys that automated tools use to send results in. Change them whenever you like from the project settings.
items.notes: better-auth apiKey plugin. Rotate UI in Project Settings · API keys. Remaining ergonomics: last-used timestamp column on keys (one column + update on auth), per-key scope (feature / feature group) — schema change, lower priority than last-used.

items.title: Available in English and French
items.summary: The interface follows your browser language. Switch any time from your account.
items.notes: Paraglide. messages/en.json + fr.json. Inlang project config in project.inlang.

items.title: Install on your own server in one command
items.summary: Run a single command and trace is up. No subscription, no account, your data stays on your machine.
items.notes: curl -fsSL <url> | docker compose -f - up -d (pipe-stdin, portable). Default TRACE_AUTH_SECRET=change-the-secret-for-prod (loud enough that anyone exposing the instance will rotate). Copy button on every install snippet. Multi-stage Node Dockerfile (build → slim runtime, @sveltejs/adapter-node). Compose brings up app + postgres + minio + minio-init with persistent volumes (pgdata, miniodata). Env via .env beside compose (TRACE_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL, DATABASE_URL, S3 creds, PORT). Lazy env init (db/client.ts + auth.ts are Proxies — connection/betterAuth() fire on first access, not on import) so production build needs zero env placeholders. Auth env renamed BETTER_AUTH_SECRET → TRACE_AUTH_SECRET (one-release deprecated fallback with console.warn). Compose hosted at trace.io repo public/compose.yml. GHCR image ghcr.io/moq77111113/trace:main (latest pending v* tag — see [[ghcr-release-tag]]).

items.title: Write tests you run by hand too
items.summary: Some tests are not automated, you run them by hand. trace lets you write those tests in the same place as the rest, alongside the Gherkin ones.
items.notes: AND model, not OR: each feature can carry Gherkin AND a free-form description AND a list of manual scenarios. Edition shipped (MR #14): description column (nullable text), manual_scenarios relational table (id, feature_id, position, name, archived) seeded with uuidv7 + check position >= 1, partial unique index on (feature_id, position) where archived=false. Feature editor refactored into three collapsible sections (Description / Manual scenarios / Gherkin) with per-feature session-scoped open state via persistedToggle factory. createFeature no longer auto-fills the Gherkin template; features start with empty content. saveFeature persists description alongside content. Manual scenarios CRUD via SvelteKit form actions (addManualScenario, renameManualScenario, archiveManualScenario, reorderManualScenarios) + optimistic client controller. Execution of manual scenarios in cockpit is a follow-up (feat/manual-features-execution) — reuses scenario_results with scenario_source enum ('GHERKIN' | 'MANUAL'), merging parsed Gherkin scenarios with active manual scenarios at run start. Out of scope for this entry: rich-text WYSIWYG, step parameterization, drag-reorder UI (controller API exists).

items.title: Use tags to organise tests
items.summary: Add labels like "smoke" or "login" to your tests. Then filter, group, or rename them from one place across the whole tool.
items.notes: Surface scenario @tags from featureContentAtStart on execution finish, persist to scenario_results.tags (text[], denormalized for query speed, indexed). Add tag filter to executions history + feature list (URL params same shape as date/status filters; chips in history rows and feature cards). Per-project Tags management page: list, rename, merge, delete-with-reassignment (today tags are created implicitly; this page makes them governable). Command palette: '#smoke' searches tags. Tag column on CSV / HTML report rows. Out of scope: tag colors, hierarchical tags, tag-based [[pbac]] conditions (the PBAC condition jsonb slot already covers it once that lands).

items.title: Reports you can print or share
items.summary: A clean summary of a run that you can hand to a client or a manager. Print it, or save it as a PDF.
items.notes: Self-contained HTML per execution + filtered set. Per-execution header: project name, feature name + code (see [[feature-codes]]), environment, branch@commit, started/finished, run by, total duration. Summary tile: total/pass/fail/skip/pass rate + distribution bar (same shape as in history). Per-scenario block: status pill, steps with per-step status, error/log on failure, attachments rendered inline (image preview, download link otherwise). Footer: trace version, generated-at timestamp, optional signature line. Print stylesheet → browser Save-as-PDF for the first cut (no headless-Chrome). Endpoints: GET /projects/:pid/executions/:eid/report.html (single), GET /projects/:pid/executions/report.html?<filters> (aggregated over same filter chain as CSV export). PBAC action: execution.read covers it (no new action). Pairs with [[campaigns]] at campaign level as the IVV-readable artefact. Out of scope first cut: signed reports, watermarking, custom per-project branding, scheduled email delivery.

items.title: Decide who can do what
items.summary: Choose, project by project, who can read, run, or change tests. Lets you safely show your work to a client without giving them edit rights.
items.notes: PBAC, not RBAC — policies as data, evaluated at request time (no role names baked into schema/UI). policies (id, subject_kind, subject_id, action, resource_kind, resource_id nullable, condition jsonb nullable, effect, priority, created_at, created_by). Indexed on (subject_kind, subject_id, resource_kind, resource_id). Subject: user id (later: group id, api-key id). Closed action enum, one entry per real verb (feature.read, feature.write, execution.start, execution.read, execution.write, project.admin, …). Resource: project | feature | execution (id or '*' for project-wide). Condition jsonb keeps ABAC door open ({tag: 'smoke'}, {environment: 'staging'}). Effect allow|deny; deny overrides allow. Default-deny on any resource with a matching policy; default-allow stays for instance admin (built-in '*' policy). Single can(user, action, resource) → bool evaluator. Bootstrap: project creator gets allow * on that project; instance admin keeps built-in *. UI: per-project Access page lists subjects + effective actions with a '+ policy' form (action picker + optional condition); no role labels in the UI — actions speak for themselves. Out of scope first cut: group subjects, policy templates, audit log of policy changes (small follow-up).

items.title: Group all tests for a release
items.summary: Bundle every test that needs to pass for a release in one place. Track progress as the team works through it. Lock the result once it's signed off.
items.notes: campaigns (id, project_id, name, app_version nullable, status planned|running|closed, opened_at, closed_at nullable, created_by). campaign_features (campaign_id, feature_id, position, required boolean). executions.campaign_id nullable: links manual or CI execution to a campaign. Absorbs per-execution app_version column — do not add it standalone (collapses the earlier cheap-first-cut for release tracking). Surfaces: project Campaigns tab (list with status pills + progress: executed/passing/total required), campaign detail (ordered feature list, last execution per feature, 'start manual run' inline). Closure snapshots results, locks the campaign — pairs with [[html-reports]] at campaign level as the IVV-readable artefact. CI ingest: optional X-CI-Campaign-Id header attaches the ingested execution to a campaign. Out of scope first cut: campaign templates, milestones across campaigns, gantt-style sequencing, campaign-level branching.

items.title: Compare two runs side by side
items.summary: Pick any two past runs of the same test. See at a glance which steps changed verdict between them.
items.notes: Fresh diff view, not in design bundle. Picker (two runs of the same feature) + table of scenarios that flipped pass<->fail between the two. Brief explicit: 'filtres (…), comparaison.'

items.title: Get a Slack message when a run ends
items.summary: When a run finishes (or fails), trace can drop a message into Slack, Teams, or anywhere else your team hangs out.
items.notes: Push notifications to external systems on execution events (finished, failed, flake detected). Replaces typical Zephyr/Slack-bot setups. webhooks (project_id, url, secret, events[], active) + webhook_deliveries (webhook_id, payload, response_code, attempted_at). Per-project URLs 1..N, optional event filter (on: ['finished', 'failed']). HMAC-signed payload with project secret so receivers can verify. Retry on 5xx with exponential backoff, give up after N attempts. Delivery log surface in project settings for debugging. Out of scope first cut: per-feature webhooks, custom payload templates, OAuth-based receivers.

items.title: Jump from a failing test to its code
items.summary: Click straight from a step that failed to the part of the project where that step lives. Faster bug hunting.
items.notes: Goal: from a failing scenario, jump to the matching .feature file + line, and on to the step definitions backing it. Also enables 'this scenario failed in 3 of the last 10 commits on main' (links executions back to source-control state, overlaps [[ci-metadata]]). Three sub-shapes, ordered cheapest to costliest: (1) X-CI-Repo-Url + X-CI-Commit + X-CI-Feature-Path ingest headers + 'View on GitHub' link next to each cockpit scenario — cheap, no schema beyond ci-metadata; (2) consumer CI uploads stepDefinitions.json (regex/expression → file:line) alongside cucumber.json — trace renders 'implemented at src/steps/login.steps.ts:42' per scenario step (medium); (3) source-of-truth toggle: when the .feature file in the repo drifts from featureContentAtExecution snapshot, surface a diff pill ('this execution ran an older revision of the feature') — helps QAs understand stale results. Out of scope first cut: bidirectional sync (editing in trace pushes back to the repo), AST-level step-definition discovery.

items.title: Polished sign in / sign up
items.summary: A cleaner welcome screen so the very first impression of trace feels finished.
items.notes: Better sign in / sign up page. Replace the current minimal form with a layout that matches the rest of the app (theme tokens, accent, OIDC button affordances). No scope decisions locked yet — design pass before scoping.

items.title: Public demo instance
items.summary: Reachable on the open internet, real instance for prospects to play with.
items.notes: One Node + one Postgres on Fly.io / Render / Hetzner. Reuse TRACE_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL. Seed demo project on install.

items.title: Trace tests itself for the demo
items.summary: Demo data comes from a real cucumber suite testing trace itself, not fabricated content.
items.notes: Don't fabricate a synthetic cucumber project — write a small Cucumber suite that exercises trace's own behavior, and let CI push those results to the demo on every commit. Landing can claim 'trace tests itself with itself' without lying. e2e/self-demo/ folder (separate from vitest, which stays the unit/integration harness). Stack: @cucumber/cucumber (cucumber-js) runner, Playwright for UI-level steps (sign-in, feature CRUD, cockpit marking, evidence upload), fetch for API-level steps (CI ingest, key auth). Feature set covers all run states naturally: auth/signin, projects/create, features/edit, runs/live-cockpit, runs/ci-ingest, import-export/roundtrip. Output cucumber.json per feature, uploaded as CI artifact (so PRs show their runs) AND pushed to demo instance.

items.title: GitHub Actions push to demo on every commit
items.summary: Every merge to main produces a live run on the demo instance.
items.notes: .github/workflows/demo-runs.yml. Triggers: push to main (every merged commit becomes a real run in the demo), pull_request (PR-scoped env=pr-<n>), workflow_dispatch (manual replay for screenshots / sales), schedule cron '0 */6 * * *' only if push activity is too sparse to keep the dashboard alive. Steps: build app, bring up Postgres + trace server, run e2e/self-demo Cucumber suite with JSON formatter, then curl -X POST each cucumber.json to https://<demo-host>/api/runs/ingest with secrets.TRACE_DEMO_API_KEY + environment header (ci / pr-<n>). One curl per feature so history shows multiple rows per commit.

items.title: Wire landing CTAs to live demo
items.summary: Replace APP_URL placeholder with the real demo URL on the landing.
items.notes: Replace prototype's APP_URL placeholder with the real demo cockpit URL. Add 'Try the live cockpit →' button in the hero. Depends on [[demo-instance]].

items.title: Record cockpit walkthrough for landing
items.summary: Replace the skeleton placeholder in the landing's Live Demo section with a real recording.
items.notes: Landing's 'Live cockpit' section ships as a skeleton placeholder today (Gherkin on the left, scenario list on the right, hand-coded status pills, 'recording soon' tag). Replace with real recording so the section stops hedging. Capture path (planned via chrome-devtools MCP): drive a clean demo project end-to-end — open cockpit, walk a few scenarios (pass / fail / skip), attach evidence on the fail, finish. Output ~15-25s loop, webm or gif sized to card width, plus a poster frame for first paint. Drop asset in trace.io public/cockpit-walkthrough.webm; swap skeleton in src/components/LiveDemo.astro for <video autoplay muted loop playsinline poster=...>. Optional polish: on-card Replay button resetting currentTime — not interactive (no Svelte/React island), just media.

items.title: Cut v0.1.0-mvp release tag
items.summary: Push a versioned tag to publish :latest on GHCR.
items.notes: Workflow already publishes :main on push and :latest on v*.*.* tag. Just need the tag + GitHub release notes + image push. Unlocks landing eyebrow 'v0.1.0-mvp' chip in the hero, image pinning in [[self-host-one-liner]], and [[trace-readme-quickstart]].

items.title: Switch trace README to one-compose path
items.summary: Once GHCR :latest exists, replace the dev-loop quickstart with the one-compose path.
items.notes: Once [[ghcr-release-tag]] lands and GHCR :latest exists, replace the current dev-loop quickstart in trace's README with the one-compose path (see [[self-host-one-liner]]). Dev-loop moves to CONTRIBUTING.md.

items.title: Pause an in-flight run
items.summary: PAUSED status alongside RUNNING. Deferred when [[abort-run]] shipped Abort only.
items.notes: Deferred per the roadmap. Revisit after [[manual-features]] ships.

items.title: Surface step-definition bindings
items.summary: Out of scope: that is a Cucumber-runtime concern, not trace's mandate.
items.notes: Partially overlaps with [[source-traceability]]'s stepDefinitions.json sub-shape.

items.title: Activity rail on the dashboard
items.summary: Live activity feed on the dashboard. Past MVP.
items.notes: Deferred per the roadmap.

items.title: Multi-section project settings
items.summary: Environments / Members / Notifications / Integrations subnav. Past MVP.
items.notes: Deferred per the roadmap; revisit alongside PBAC + webhooks.

items.title: Trace CLI
items.summary: trace init / push / export. Out of scope for MVP.
items.notes: CI integration is curl + cucumber.json, already shipped. Designer's CLI snippets dropped in favour of curl on the landing.
